title: The pause between breaths
subtitle: A short evening visualisation that uses the natural pause in the breath cycle as a doorway into stillness.
excerpt: A five-minute visualisation from Day 19 of the SLEEP program. The practice uses a single image: the natural, brief pause between the exhale and the next inhale. That pause is safe. It has always been safe.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Creative visualisation as a self-development tool draws on public-domain traditions.

content.text: A five-minute visualisation from the Day 19 theme. The image is the pause between breaths: the brief stillness that exists in every breath cycle, whether the mind registers it or not.
Lie down. Close your eyes. Let the breath be natural, don't control it.

After a few breaths, start to notice the pause that comes at the end of the exhale. It is very short. Before the body draws the next breath in, there is a moment of stillness. Just a moment. Nothing happening. The lungs neither full nor empty. The breath between stopped.

Notice that you are completely safe in that pause. It is the same pause that has been there in every breath of your life. The next breath has always come. The pause has never been a gap that didn't close.
Rest your attention in that pause for a few cycles. Exhale, pause, inhale, exhale, pause. The pause is safe. The stillness is safe. Nothing is required of you in it.
Let the pause get a little longer if the body allows it. Not forcing, just noticing whether the body is willing to rest in the stillness a moment more.
Stay with this for two to three minutes. Then let the breath return to its natural pace, and sleep.
This piece is original to homemade.education. Creative imagery as a self-help tool has a long history in the public domain.
